title: Cephalomanes clathratum
intro: Cephalomanes clathratum, also known as the net-veined polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephalomanes clathratum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cephalomanes clathratum is often prepared as an infusion or a decoction, and is commonly used in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Cephalomanes clathratum is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the order Hymenophytaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is characterized by its unique, lacy fronds. The classification of Cephalomanes clathratum is further divided into several subcategories, including its genus, species, family, order, and class. In the botanical hierarchy, it falls under the class Polypodiopsida.
